nodes.context: Yakuza organization based in Tokyo, Japan, designated yakuza group with about 280 members as of 2024.
Yakuza fraternal federation based in Kantō region, includes Matsuba-kai, Sumiyoshi-kai, Inagawa-kai, Toa-kai, and Soai-kai.
Kantō-based yakuza syndicate, member of Kantō Hatsuka-kai.
Kantō-based yakuza syndicate, member of Kantō Hatsuka-kai.
Kantō-based yakuza syndicate, member of Kantō Hatsuka-kai.
Kantō-based yakuza syndicate, member of Kantō Hatsuka-kai.
Precursor yakuza gang to Matsuba-kai, founded in 1936 by Masaru Sekine, disbanded in 1947.
Yakuza gang whose remnants merged with Sekine-gumi remnants to form Matsuba-kai in 1953.
Rival yakuza group involved in a violent feud with Matsuba-kai in early 2000s.

edges.evidence_quote: The Matsuba-kai is a member of a yakuza fraternal federation named the Kantō Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kantō -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Toa-kai , and the Soai-kai .
edges.context: Matsuba-kai is a member of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Matsuba-kai is a member of a yakuza fraternal federation named the Kantō Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kantō -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Toa-kai , and the Soai-kai .
edges.context: Matsuba-kai and Sumiyoshi-kai are fellow members of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Matsuba-kai is a member of a yakuza fraternal federation named the Kantō Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kantō -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Toa-kai , and the Soai-kai .
edges.context: Matsuba-kai and Inagawa-kai are fellow members of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Matsuba-kai is a member of a yakuza fraternal federation named the Kantō Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kantō -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Toa-kai , and the Soai-kai .
edges.context: Matsuba-kai and Toa-kai are fellow members of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: The Matsuba-kai is a member of a yakuza fraternal federation named the Kantō Hatsuka-kai, along with four other Kantō -based yakuza syndicates, the Sumiyoshi-kai , the Inagawa-kai , the Toa-kai , and the Soai-kai .
edges.context: Matsuba-kai and Soai-kai are fellow members of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation.

edges.evidence_quote: The precursor to the Matsuba-kai was the Sekine-gumi ( 関根組 ) , a yakuza gang founded in Sumida, Tokyo in 1936 by a bakuto named Masaru Sekine.
edges.context: Sekine-gumi was the precursor to Matsuba-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: remnants of the gang came together with the remnants of another gang, the Fujita-gumi, to found a new organization called the "Matsuba-kai" in March 1953.
edges.context: Remnants of Sekine-gumi and Fujita-gumi merged to form Matsuba-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2000s the Matsuba-kai was involved in a violent feud with the rival Kyokuto-kai , which led to a number of shootings.
edges.context: Matsuba-kai and Kyokuto-kai were involved in a violent feud in the early 2000s.
